prompt: Point to yourself. How do you say 'I' or 'me'?
ipaHint: Note if subject and object forms differ

prompt: Say: 'He sees me.' How do you say 'me' as the object?
ipaHint: Note if same as subject form 'I'

prompt: Point to the speaker. How do you say 'you'?
ipaHint: Note if formal/informal distinction exists

prompt: Point to a man. How do you say 'he' or 'him'?
ipaHint: Note if gender is marked on pronoun

prompt: Point to a woman. How do you say 'she' or 'her'?
ipaHint: Note if same as masculine form

prompt: Point to an object. How do you say 'it'?
ipaHint: Note if same as animate pronoun

prompt: Point to yourself and the person you're speaking to. How do you say 'we' including the listener?
ipaHint: Clusivity: inclusive vs exclusive 'we' is a key typological feature

prompt: Point to yourself and others but not the listener. How do you say 'we' not including the listener?
ipaHint: If clusivity is not marked, record the same form as gram_007

prompt: Point to a group of people. How do you say 'you all'?
ipaHint: Note if distinct from singular 'you'

prompt: Point to a group. How do you say 'they' or 'them'?
ipaHint: Note animate vs inanimate distinction

prompt: Point to something near you. How do you say 'this'?
ipaHint: Note proximity system — 2-way or 3-way?

prompt: Point to something far away. How do you say 'that'?
ipaHint: Note if medial/far distinction exists

prompt: Point to the ground near your feet. How do you say 'here'?
ipaHint: May be related to proximal demonstrative

prompt: Point somewhere distant. How do you say 'there'?
ipaHint: May distinguish near-hearer vs far-from-both

prompt: How do you ask 'who is that?'
ipaHint: Note wh-movement: does it front or stay in place?

prompt: Hold up an unfamiliar object. How do you ask 'what is this?'
ipaHint: Note if same root as 'who'

prompt: How do you ask 'where are you going?'
ipaHint: Note locative morphology

prompt: How do you ask 'when will you come?'
ipaHint: Note tense marking on question

prompt: How do you ask 'how did you do that?'
ipaHint: Note if distinct from 'how many'

prompt: How do you ask 'why did you do that?'
ipaHint: Note causal morphology

prompt: Hold two objects and ask: how do you say 'which one do you want?'
ipaHint: May inflect for gender or class

prompt: Show fingers and ask 'how many?'
ipaHint: Note if singular/plural distinction applies

prompt: How do you say 'I am here' or 'there is water'?
ipaHint: Note if copula and existential are distinct verbs

prompt: Show something you own. How do you say 'I have this'?
ipaHint: Note if possession is expressed via verb or locative construction

prompt: Mime making something. How do you say 'I make' or 'I do'?
ipaHint: Note if 'do' and 'make' are distinct

prompt: Walk away a few steps. How do you say 'I go'?
ipaHint: Note directional morphology

prompt: Walk toward the speaker. How do you say 'I come'?
ipaHint: Note if 'come' and 'go' share a root

prompt: Hand something to someone. How do you say 'give me that'?
ipaHint: Classic ditransitive — note argument structure

prompt: Take an object from someone. How do you say 'I take'?
ipaHint: Note if same root as 'receive'

prompt: Mime speaking. How do you say 'I say' or 'I speak'?
ipaHint: Note if 'say' and 'speak' are distinct

prompt: Point to your eyes. How do you say 'I see'?
ipaHint: Note if perception and directed gaze are distinct

prompt: Cup your ear. How do you say 'I hear'?
ipaHint: Note if 'hear' and 'listen' are distinct

prompt: Mime eating. How do you say 'I eat'?
ipaHint: Note object-marking when food is specified

prompt: Mime drinking. How do you say 'I drink'?
ipaHint: Note if same root as water

prompt: Close your eyes and mime sleeping. How do you say 'I sleep'?
ipaHint: Note if same root as 'dream'

prompt: Stand up. How do you say 'I stand'?
ipaHint: Note postural verbs — they vary widely

prompt: Sit down. How do you say 'I sit'?
ipaHint: Note postural verbs

prompt: Walk slowly in place. How do you say 'I walk'?
ipaHint: Note path vs manner distinctions

prompt: Mime running. How do you say 'I run'?
ipaHint: Note if related to 'fast'

prompt: Mime dying. How do you say 'he dies'?
ipaHint: Note taboo avoidance — may use euphemism

prompt: How do you say 'he killed the animal'?
ipaHint: Note causative relationship to 'die'

prompt: How do you say 'he is still alive'?
ipaHint: Note if same root as 'life'

prompt: Point to something desirable. How do you say 'I want this'?
ipaHint: Note mood marking with want-verbs

prompt: Tap your head. How do you say 'I know'?
ipaHint: Note if know-person and know-fact are distinct

prompt: Mime thinking. How do you say 'I think'?
ipaHint: Note propositional complement structure

prompt: Put your hand on your heart. How do you say 'I feel'?
ipaHint: Note if physical and emotional feeling are distinct

prompt: How do you say 'I like this' or 'I love you'?
ipaHint: Note if gradable — like vs love distinction

prompt: Mime working with hands. How do you say 'I work'?
ipaHint: Note if related to 'hard' or 'effort'

prompt: Open a door or container. How do you say 'open it'?
ipaHint: Note if transitive and intransitive are distinct

prompt: Close a door or container. How do you say 'close it'?
ipaHint: Note causative relationship to 'closed'

prompt: Put an object on a surface. How do you say 'put it there'?
ipaHint: Note if placement verb varies by surface type

prompt: Receive something. How do you say 'I get' or 'I received'?
ipaHint: Note if same as 'take'

prompt: Pretend to find something hidden. How do you say 'I found it'?
ipaHint: Note telic/resultative aspect

prompt: Carry something toward the speaker. How do you say 'bring it here'?
ipaHint: Note if directional suffix marks toward speaker

prompt: Carry an object. How do you say 'I carry'?
ipaHint: Note if carry-on-back vs carry-in-arms are distinct

prompt: Mime something falling. How do you say 'it fell'?
ipaHint: Note animate vs inanimate distinction

prompt: Rise from a seated position. How do you say 'I got up'?
ipaHint: Note if same as 'stand'

prompt: Turn around. How do you say 'turn around'?
ipaHint: Note if directional affixes mark direction

prompt: Hold up a hand in a waiting gesture. How do you say 'wait'?
ipaHint: Note if related to 'stay'

prompt: Mime helping someone lift something. How do you say 'help me'?
ipaHint: Note benefactive marking

prompt: How do you say 'I asked him'?
ipaHint: Note if question morpheme is same as verb

prompt: How do you say 'I answered'?
ipaHint: Note if related to 'say' or 'speak'

prompt: Point to a man, mime seeing, point to a woman. Say the full sentence.
ipaHint: Note word order: S-V-O? S-O-V? V-S-O?

english: The man does not see the woman.
prompt: Same as above but with negation.
ipaHint: Note negation strategy: particle? prefix? suffix? auxiliary?

prompt: How do you say this in the past?
ipaHint: Note tense/aspect marker position

english: The man will see the woman.
prompt: Point forward to indicate future. How do you say this?
ipaHint: Note future marker — same as irrealis?

english: Does the man see the woman?
prompt: Ask this as a yes/no question.
ipaHint: Note question formation: intonation only? particle? word order change?

prompt: How do you ask this wh-question?
ipaHint: Does 'who' move to front, or stay in object position?

prompt: Hold up your hand. How do you say 'this is my hand'?
ipaHint: Note possessive morphology — prefix, suffix, or separate word?

prompt: Point to water nearby. How do you say 'there is water here'?
ipaHint: Note existential construction — same as copula?

prompt: Gesture to come toward you. How do you give this command?
ipaHint: Note imperative morphology

prompt: Gesture a stop. How do you say 'don't go'?
ipaHint: Note prohibitive — same as declarative negation or different?

prompt: Hold up one finger and say 'one fish', then two. Does the word 'fish' change?
ipaHint: Note singular/plural marking on nouns

prompt: Describe a big red house. What order do the adjectives go in?
ipaHint: Note adjective-noun order and modifier stacking

prompt: Hold two objects of different sizes. How do you say 'this is bigger than that'?
ipaHint: Note comparative strategy — suffix, particle, or separate construction?

prompt: How do you say 'she caused him to eat'?
ipaHint: Note causative morphology — productive suffix? periphrastic?

english: If it rains, I will stay.
prompt: How do you say this conditional sentence?
ipaHint: Note conditional marker and mood on apodosis

prompt: Touch your head. How do you say 'head'?
Pull your hair lightly. How do you say 'hair'?
ipaHint: Note if head hair vs body hair are distinct

prompt: Point to your eye. How do you say 'eye'?
Touch your ear. How do you say 'ear'?
Point to your nose. How do you say 'nose'?
Point to your mouth. How do you say 'mouth'?
Show your teeth. How do you say 'tooth'?
ipaHint: Note singular vs plural form

prompt: Stick out your tongue. How do you say 'tongue'?
Show your fingernail. How do you say 'fingernail'?
ipaHint: Note if toenail is same word

prompt: Point to your foot. How do you say 'foot'?
ipaHint: Note if same root as 'leg'

prompt: Bend and point to your knee. How do you say 'knee'?
Hold up your hand. How do you say 'hand'?
ipaHint: Note if same root as 'arm' or distinct

prompt: Pat your stomach. How do you say 'belly'?
Touch your neck. How do you say 'neck'?
Touch your back. How do you say 'back'?
ipaHint: Note if same as 'behind' spatial term

prompt: Touch your chest. How do you say 'chest' or 'breast'?
ipaHint: Note if male chest vs female breast are distinct

prompt: Put your hand on your heart. How do you say 'heart'?
ipaHint: Note if emotional vs anatomical heart are distinct

prompt: Point to your liver area. How do you say 'liver'?
ipaHint: Liver has symbolic importance in many cultures

prompt: Show a small scratch or cut. How do you say 'blood'?
Point to a knuckle. How do you say 'bone'?
Pinch the skin on your arm. How do you say 'skin'?
ipaHint: Note if same word as 'bark' of tree

prompt: Pinch some body fat. How do you say 'fat'?
ipaHint: Note if cooking grease uses same word

prompt: Show an egg or mime one. How do you say 'egg'?
Make horns with your fingers. How do you say 'horn' of an animal?
Point to where a tail would be on an animal. How do you say 'tail'?
Show a feather or mime one. How do you say 'feather'?
ipaHint: Note if same as 'wing'

prompt: Flap your arms like wings. How do you say 'wing'?
Mime a fish swimming. How do you say 'fish'?
ipaHint: Note if a generic word exists or only species names

prompt: Flap your arms briefly. How do you say 'bird'?
ipaHint: Note if generic term or specific species

prompt: Mime a dog. How do you say 'dog'?
Mime picking lice. How do you say 'louse'?
ipaHint: Very stable cross-linguistically

prompt: Make a snake motion with your hand. How do you say 'snake'?
ipaHint: Note any avoidance taboo on the word

prompt: Mime a worm. How do you say 'worm'?
Mime tiny insects marching. How do you say 'ant'?
Swat at an imaginary fly. How do you say 'fly' (the insect)?
ipaHint: Note if same root as verb 'to fly'

prompt: Mime a small scurrying rodent. How do you say 'mouse' or 'rat'?
ipaHint: Note if mouse and rat are distinct

prompt: Point to a piece of meat. How do you say 'meat'?
ipaHint: Note if 'meat' and 'flesh' are same word

prompt: Point up toward the sun. How do you say 'sun'?
ipaHint: Note if same as 'day' or 'light'

prompt: Point to where the moon would be. How do you say 'moon'?
ipaHint: Note if same as 'month'

prompt: Point up at the sky at night. How do you say 'star'?
Pour or point to water. How do you say 'water'?
ipaHint: Core Swadesh item — extremely stable

prompt: Wiggle fingers downward like rain. How do you say 'rain'?
ipaHint: Note if noun and verb are distinct

prompt: Mime flowing water. How do you say 'river'?
ipaHint: Note if same as 'stream' or 'water'

prompt: Gesture widely to indicate a large body of water. How do you say 'sea'?
ipaHint: Note if inland speakers use this term

prompt: Pick up or point to a stone. How do you say 'stone'?
ipaHint: Note if small stone and large rock are distinct

prompt: Mime scooping sand. How do you say 'sand'?
Touch the ground. How do you say 'earth' or 'ground'?
ipaHint: Note if same as 'land' or 'world'

prompt: Point to clouds. How do you say 'cloud'?
Mime smoke rising. How do you say 'smoke'?
ipaHint: Note if same as 'steam' or 'fog'

prompt: Mime fire with hands. How do you say 'fire'?
ipaHint: Note if related to 'burn' or 'cook'

prompt: Rub fingers together as if feeling ash. How do you say 'ash'?
Point to a tree. How do you say 'tree'?
ipaHint: Note if generic term or only species names

prompt: Mime pulling a root from the ground. How do you say 'root'?
Hold up a leaf. How do you say 'leaf'?
Touch tree bark. How do you say 'bark' of a tree?
ipaHint: Note if same word as 'skin'

prompt: Hold up a flower. How do you say 'flower'?
Point to or pick grass. How do you say 'grass'?
ipaHint: Note if same as 'plant' or 'weed'

prompt: Mime holding a rope. How do you say 'rope'?
Mime sleeping, point to a dark sky. How do you say 'night'?
ipaHint: Note if same as 'dark'

prompt: Point to the sun. How do you say 'day'?
ipaHint: Note if same as 'sun' or '24-hour period'

prompt: Make a circular gesture to indicate a cycle. How do you say 'year'?
ipaHint: Note if related to 'season' or 'harvest'

prompt: Mime wind blowing. How do you say 'wind'?
ipaHint: Note if same as 'breath' or 'air'

prompt: Mime snow falling. How do you say 'snow'? (Ask if applicable)
ipaHint: May not be in speaker's experience

prompt: Mime something cold and hard like ice. How do you say 'ice'?
ipaHint: May not be in speaker's experience

prompt: Blow on your hand to show dust. How do you say 'dust'?
ipaHint: Note if same as 'sand' or 'ash'

prompt: Point along a path. How do you say 'path' or 'road'?
ipaHint: Note if foot-path and road are distinct

prompt: Mime biting. How do you say 'bite'?
ipaHint: Note if related to 'tooth'

prompt: Mime spitting. How do you say 'spit'?
Mime vomiting. How do you say 'vomit'?
Blow air from your mouth. How do you say 'blow'?
ipaHint: Note if breath-blowing and wind-blowing are distinct

prompt: Breathe deeply. How do you say 'breathe'?
ipaHint: Note if related to 'breath' noun or 'wind'

prompt: Mime laughing. How do you say 'laugh'?
Mime crying. How do you say 'cry'?
ipaHint: Note if same as 'call out'

prompt: Sniff the air. How do you say 'I smell something'?
ipaHint: Note if same verb for smell and sniff

prompt: Show a frightened expression. How do you say 'I am afraid'?
ipaHint: Note if stative verb or noun-based construction

prompt: Mime fighting. How do you say 'fight'?
ipaHint: Note reciprocal marking on verb

prompt: Mime stalking game. How do you say 'hunt'?
ipaHint: Note if same as 'search' or 'chase'

prompt: Mime children playing. How do you say 'play'?
ipaHint: Note if same as 'game'

prompt: Mime something floating on water. How do you say 'float'?
ipaHint: Note if same as 'swim' for humans

prompt: Mime water flowing. How do you say 'flow'?
ipaHint: Note if same as 'river'

prompt: Mime something turning solid from cold. How do you say 'freeze'?
ipaHint: Note if same as 'cold'

prompt: Puff up your cheek. How do you say 'swell'?
Mime fire burning. How do you say 'burn'?
ipaHint: Note transitive vs intransitive

prompt: Scratch your arm. How do you say 'scratch'?
Mime digging. How do you say 'dig'?
Mime swimming. How do you say 'swim'?
ipaHint: Note if same as 'float'

prompt: Flap arms like wings. How do you say 'fly'?
ipaHint: Note if same root as 'bird'

prompt: Mime lying on the ground. How do you say 'lie down'?
ipaHint: Note if same as 'sleep'

prompt: Grip an object tightly. How do you say 'hold'?
ipaHint: Note if force of grip is encoded in verb

prompt: Squeeze something in your fist. How do you say 'squeeze'?
Rub your hands together. How do you say 'rub'?
ipaHint: Note if same as 'wash'

prompt: Mime washing hands. How do you say 'wash'?
ipaHint: Note if object-specific

prompt: Mime wiping a surface. How do you say 'wipe'?
Mime pulling a rope. How do you say 'pull'?
ipaHint: Note directional morphology

prompt: Mime pushing something. How do you say 'push'?
Mime throwing a ball. How do you say 'throw'?
ipaHint: Note direction/target encoding

prompt: Mime tying a knot. How do you say 'tie'?
Mime sewing. How do you say 'sew'?
Count on your fingers. How do you say 'count'?
ipaHint: Note if related to number words

prompt: Hum or sing a note. How do you say 'sing'?
ipaHint: Note if same as 'song'

prompt: Mime cutting with a knife. How do you say 'cut'?
ipaHint: Note if different verb for cutting food vs rope

prompt: Mime splitting wood. How do you say 'split'?
ipaHint: Note if same as 'cut'

prompt: Place an object in a cup. How do you say 'the stone is in the cup'?
ipaHint: Note if locative is a preposition, postposition, or case

prompt: How do you say 'I cut with a knife'? And 'I went with him'?
ipaHint: Note if comitative and instrumental use same marker

prompt: Hold up two objects. How do you say 'the stone and the water'?
ipaHint: Note if NP and clause conjunction differ

prompt: How do you say 'if it rains'?
ipaHint: Note mood/aspect changes in conditional clauses

prompt: How do you say 'because I was tired'?
ipaHint: Note clause-linking strategy

prompt: Shake your head. How do you express negation?
ipaHint: Note position: preverbal, postverbal, circumfix?

prompt: Sweep your arm to include everything. How do you say 'all'?
ipaHint: Note if modifies nouns vs verbs differently

prompt: Show many fingers. How do you say 'many'?
ipaHint: Note if count and mass are distinct

prompt: Take a handful of something. How do you say 'some'?
Hold up 2 or 3 fingers. How do you say 'few'?
ipaHint: Note if same as 'small'

prompt: After showing one thing, point to a different one. How do you say 'another'?
Point to your leg. How do you say 'leg'?
ipaHint: Note if thigh and lower leg are distinct

prompt: Touch your shoulder. How do you say 'shoulder'?
Touch your arm. How do you say 'arm'?
ipaHint: Note if same as 'hand' or distinct

prompt: Point to your navel. How do you say 'navel'?
ipaHint: Very stable cross-linguistically

prompt: Make a bitter face. How do you say 'bitter'?
ipaHint: Taste adjectives are culturally salient

prompt: Mime eating something sweet. How do you say 'sweet'?
ipaHint: Note if same as 'good' or 'delicious'

prompt: Lift a heavy object. How do you say 'heavy'?
Show something muddy. How do you say 'dirty'?
ipaHint: Note if moral and physical dirty are distinct

prompt: Run a finger carefully along a blade edge. How do you say 'sharp'?
ipaHint: Note if same as 'pointed'

prompt: Touch a wooden object. How do you say 'wood'?
ipaHint: Note if same as 'tree'

prompt: Show salt. How do you say 'salt'?
ipaHint: Cross-linguistically stable — often a trade item

prompt: Mime a pointed peak. How do you say 'mountain'?
ipaHint: Note if mountain and hill are distinct

prompt: Point to something red. How do you say 'red'?
ipaHint: Often same root as 'blood'

prompt: Point to leaves (green) and fruit (yellow). Are these the same word or different?
ipaHint: Green-yellow merger is common cross-linguistically

prompt: Stretch your arms wide. How do you say 'long'?
ipaHint: Note if spatial and temporal 'long' use same word

prompt: Hold up one finger. How do you say 'one'?
ipaHint: Note if used as an article

prompt: Hold up two fingers. How do you say 'two'?
ipaHint: Note dual number — may have special grammatical role

prompt: Hold up three fingers. How do you say 'three'?
Hold up four fingers. How do you say 'four'?
Hold up five fingers. How do you say 'five'?
ipaHint: Note if related to 'hand' — reveals counting base

prompt: Hold up six fingers. How do you say 'six'?
ipaHint: Note if six = five+one or independent

prompt: Hold up seven fingers. How do you say 'seven'?
Hold up eight fingers. How do you say 'eight'?
Hold up nine fingers. How do you say 'nine'?
Hold up all ten fingers. How do you say 'ten'?
ipaHint: Note if ten = two-fives or independent

prompt: How do you say 'twenty'?

prompt: How do you say 'one hundred'?
ipaHint: Note if borrowed from trade language

prompt: How do you say 'one thousand'?
ipaHint: Often borrowed

prompt: Show empty hands. How do you say 'none' or 'zero'?

prompt: Add more objects to a pile. How do you say 'more'?
ipaHint: Note if same as 'again' or 'extra'

prompt: Remove objects from a pile. How do you say 'less'?
Step forward. How do you say 'first'?
ipaHint: Note if derived from 'one' or a separate word

prompt: Point to the last item in a line. How do you say 'last'?
Point upward. How do you say 'above' or 'up'?
ipaHint: Note if same as 'sky' or 'high'

prompt: Point downward. How do you say 'below' or 'down'?
Step in front of someone. How do you say 'in front of'?
ipaHint: Note if same as temporal 'before'

prompt: Step behind someone. How do you say 'behind'?
ipaHint: Note if same as temporal 'after'

prompt: Point to your left. How do you say 'left'?
ipaHint: Note if absolute or relative directional system

prompt: Point to your right. How do you say 'right'?
ipaHint: Note if absolute or relative system

prompt: Put your hand in a bag. How do you say 'inside'?

prompt: Step out of a doorway. How do you say 'outside'?

prompt: Point south. How do you say 'south'?

prompt: Point west (toward sunset). How do you say 'west'?

prompt: Point to the zenith sun. How do you say 'afternoon'?

prompt: How do you say 'mother'?

prompt: How do you say 'father'?

prompt: How do you say 'child'? Is there a generic word?

prompt: How do you say 'son'?

prompt: How do you say 'daughter'?

prompt: How do you say 'wife'?

prompt: How do you say 'older brother'?

prompt: How do you say 'younger brother'?

prompt: Point to a friend. How do you say 'friend'?

prompt: Ask 'what is your name?' How do you say 'name'?

prompt: Stretch your arms wide. How do you say 'big'?

prompt: Hold fingers close together. How do you say 'small'?

prompt: Fan yourself. How do you say 'hot'?

prompt: Shiver. How do you say 'cold'?

ipaHint: Note if same as 'water' + suffix

prompt: Rub dry hands together. How do you say 'dry'?
Mime a full container. How do you say 'full'?
ipaHint: Note if physically full and satiated are same word

prompt: Show an empty container. How do you say 'empty'?
Give a thumbs up. How do you say 'good'?
ipaHint: Note if morally good and quality good are same word

prompt: Give a thumbs down. How do you say 'bad'?
ipaHint: Note if opposite of 'good' or a different root

prompt: Hold up something brand new. How do you say 'new'?
ipaHint: Note if same as 'young' for living things

prompt: Hold up something worn or old. How do you say 'old'?
ipaHint: Note if person-old and thing-old are distinct

prompt: Mime something moving quickly. How do you say 'fast'?
Mime something moving slowly. How do you say 'slow'?
Tap something hard. How do you say 'hard'?
ipaHint: Note if same as 'difficult'

prompt: Squeeze something soft. How do you say 'soft'?
Show clean hands. How do you say 'clean'?
ipaHint: Note if ritual clean and physical clean are distinct

prompt: Show muddy hands. How do you say 'dirty'?
Look ill and hold your stomach. How do you say 'sick'?
ipaHint: Note if same as 'pain' or 'hurt'

prompt: Flex and look healthy. How do you say 'healthy'?
Pretend to lift something very heavy. How do you say 'heavy'?
Lift something light easily. How do you say 'light' (not heavy)?
ipaHint: Note if same as light (not dark)

prompt: Point to something black. How do you say 'black'?
ipaHint: Note if same as 'dark' or 'night'

prompt: Point to something white. How do you say 'white'?
ipaHint: Note if same as 'light' or 'bright'

prompt: Point to something red. How do you say 'red'?
ipaHint: Often related to 'blood'

prompt: Point to leaves or grass. How do you say 'green'?
ipaHint: Green-yellow merger is common — test if separate or same

prompt: Point to the sky or water. How do you say 'blue'?
ipaHint: Blue-green merger is common in many language families

prompt: Point to something yellow. How do you say 'yellow'?
ipaHint: May merge with green

prompt: Point to soil or wood. How do you say 'brown'?
ipaHint: Often described via reference to wood or earth

prompt: Point to an orange fruit. How do you say 'orange'?
ipaHint: Often derived from the fruit name

prompt: Point to something purple. How do you say 'purple'?
ipaHint: May be described as dark red or dark blue

prompt: Point to something grey. How do you say 'grey'?
ipaHint: Often described via reference — ask what grey things are called
